kept.text:
$META is down nearly 10% after earnings, losing over $150 billion in market cap. 

The stock's significant decline follows a disappointing earnings report. 

$META's market cap drop is one of the largest recent losses.

Why did this happen ?

Let’s break it down.

The drop wasn’t because Meta’s earnings were bad, it was because investors ain’t sure of what’s coming next. 

The real reasons the stocks dropped 

1📌Massive Ai spending scared investors : Meta shocked the market by raising its 2026 spending (Capex) $125B - $145B

•That’s tens of billions more than expected 

•Most of it is going into Ai infrastructure (data centers,infrastructures and chips etc)

So investors are asking, when are we actually going to see returns on all this money, to them, the answer is unclear.

2📌Profit quality concerns 
Even though earnings looked strong , part of the profit came from a tax benefit .(one off boost)

To investors , future earnings may not look as strong so they are discounting today’s good numbers .

3📌Huge spending = Less cash left over : lower free cash flow + Less room for stock buy backs .
That’s bearish for stock prices in the short term .

4📌”Spend now” maybe profit “later” narrative .

This is the big one:

Meta is going all In on Ai but:
•No clear timelines for pay off
•No cloud business like Amazon or Microsoft to monetize Ai quickly .

An investor’s greatest fear is “Uncertainty”.

When you hold a stock study the company closes .

Doesn’t matter if it is already a bluechip or not.

That is what smart investors do.

kept.text:
$AMD $META Dr. Su comment🤝

"We actually see an opportunity to go much broader than that in the sense that you're actually charting the path for where AI infrastructure is going in the future.  So the value that's accrued to AMD of a deal like this is, yes, we get to accelerate purchases, which is a great thing, but we also get to accelerate our technology ecosystem, our software ecosystem that accrues benefits beyond just the work that we're doing with Meta, but really to the overall AMD ecosystem.  And the key with how we've designed these warrants is they're very, very performance-based.  So in some sense, both companies are incented to help each other win.  We win when Meta's foundational models are super successful and they need lots and lots of chips, so we are motivated to give them the best infrastructure for their workloads.  And they're motivated to ensure that our ecosystem is as strong as it can be.  And there's a very good win-win synergistic partnership.  But it is a special thing.  I think the conversation is around we want to build a very rich ecosystem."

quoted_tweet.text:
$AMD 🤝 $META to x3 from here 🧵
Not Financial Advice!

The 10 analysts downgraded $META today are as clueless as those that downgraded @AMD in 2023.

They have no idea what they downgraded. 

AMD's partnership with Meta directly supports accelerated AI infrastructure scaling, cost efficiency, and faster model deployment key drivers for Meta's long-term growth. Especially Helios Rack will bring down Inference cost to $0.0003-$0.0005/M Tokens. Read linked threads below to understand more.

1. Why Analysts are wrong?

Q1 2026 results crushed estimates: Revenue $56.3B (+33% YoY), adjusted EPS beat handily (strong net income growth even excluding tax benefits). Ad impressions and pricing powered growth.  33%. Read it again 33% at this size!!!!

- 19% YoY jump in ad impressions
- 12% YoY jump in avg price per ad
- 29% FXN YoY revenue growth

Fwd P/E multiple should be 27-30x. not 20-21x 
at this kind of growth for this size!

Meta is projected to surpass Google in global digital ad revenue in 2026 (~$243B vs. ~$240B), with faster growth (24%+ vs. Google's ~12%). AI tools like Advantage+ (end-to-end automated campaigns), Reels monetization, and generative creative features are driving higher engagement, better ROI for advertisers, and pricing power.

AI-powered optimizations have boosted clicks, conversions, and efficiency. Advantage+ and related tools have a massive revenue run-rate (previously cited in the $60B+ range in prior quarters). This isn't speculative, it's fueling the ad machine that makes up ~97% of revenue.

Mark Zuckerberg and management express high conviction in the spend, pointing to industry signals, custom chips AMD, efficiency gains, and building toward leading models/products. They expect operating income to still grow in 2026 despite higher expenses

2. How Inference cost to $0.0003-$0.0005/M Tokens Helps Meta Accelerate Growth

Cheaper AI everywhere = more usage & revenue: Meta’s ad business (~97% of revenue) runs on AI recommendations, creative tools (Advantage+), and Reels. Ultra-low inference costs let Meta run far more AI queries, personalize better, generate more content, and improve advertiser ROI driving higher engagement, ad impressions, pricing power, and growth (already +33% revenue in Q1 2026). 

Mark Zuckerberg’s vision for advanced AI features across 3.5B+ users becomes economically viable. Low costs support massive deployment without destroying margins, even as capex rises to $125–145B in 2026.
Efficient inference helps operating income grow despite higher spend.

A full Helios rack packs up to 72 custom MI450-based Instinct GPUs + 6th-gen EPYC "Venice" CPUs. It delivers massive scale-out performance (1.4 exaFLOPS FP8, up to 2.9 exaFLOPS FP4) with 31 TB+ HBM4 memory and 1.4 PB/s bandwidth per rack. This enables huge batch sizes, higher parallelism, and better utilization for inference-heavy workloads lowering cost per token vs. smaller or less integrated systems

The MI450 variant is tuned specifically for Meta’s workloads (heavy on inference, ranking/recommendations). Paired with Venice EPYC CPUs (co-optimized by Meta), it boosts tokens/second while reducing power draw and overhead. Open Compute Project  Open Rack Wide design improves cooling, networking, and serviceability, cutting TCO further. 

Conclusion: 

The long term partnership between Dr. Lisa Su and Mark Zuckerberg is a powerful catalyst accelerating Meta’s AI infrastructure advantage, powering sustainable revenue growth of 30%+potentially hitting 35%+ in key periods well ahead of most other mega-cap peers. 

 Dr. Su emphasized: “This multi-year, multi-generation collaboration... aligns our roadmaps to deliver high-performance, energy-efficient infrastructure optimized for Meta’s workloads, accelerating one of the industry’s largest AI deployments.” Zuckerberg responded: “We’re excited to form a long-term partnership with AMD to deploy efficient inference compute and deliver personal superintelligence... I expect AMD to be an important partner for many years to come.” 

Meta’s Q1 2026 results already showed +33% revenue growth (crushing estimates), with strong momentum into Q2 guidance. This outpaces typical mega-cap peers: Google/Alphabet’s ad growth in the mid-teens, slower overall revenue ramps at many others amid AI investment pressures. The leaderships alignment positions Meta to sustain or exceed 30–35%+ topline expansion as AI monetization compounds faster than most peers burdened by higher competition or less direct ad-AI flywheels. 

Analyst downgrades obsess over near-term capex optics and margin noise, but they miss the execution flywheel. The proven ad business + efficient AI scaling via partners like AMD is converting massive investment into durable competitive advantage and superior growth. History favors Meta’s big bets when they focus on user/ad value this one looks set to deliver another powerful rebound and compounding returns. The current post-earnings dip could be a compelling entry for long-term believers in Meta’s AI-augmented advertising dominance.

Not Financial Advice!

kept.text:
BREAKING: $META is down 10% even after posting strong earning report.

Revenue hit $56.31 billion, Profits came in at $10.44 per share. Both beat what Wall Street expected.

The problem is what came after the numbers.

Meta just told investors it will spend up to $145 billion this year on AI data centers and infrastructure. That is $10 billion more than what they guided just weeks ago.

For context, that is more than the entire annual revenue of most Fortune 500 companies, being spent in a single year on infrastructure that is still in early stages of generating meaningful new revenue.

And there is one more thing the headline EPS number is hiding. The $10.44 profit figure includes an $8 billion one-time tax benefit. Strip that out and actual EPS was $7.31.

kept.text:
$META - META is cheap because they trade at a forward PE of just 20.

That sounds great doesn't it?

But let's be a bit more critical here. 

I'm not sure we should be looking at the PE multiple here. I'd rather look at EV/FCF.

First, 2026 capex nearly 2x'd compared to 2025. 

But Capex doesn't show on the income statement immediately because it gets depreciated over 4–6 years.

The hit to earnings likely comes in 2027–2029.

Also, an $8B tax benefit inflated this Q1 EPS beat. 

You also have RealityLabs sittin in there with almost $20B in losses which is masked in consolidated EPS

The ROI on the AI spend is still unknown. And that will be very important to keep tracking. 

If these investments work out, the E is understated. 

If it doesn't, depreciation will certainly eat into  earnings hard. 

I think EV/FCF is a better metric to use. With this metric you actually capture the capex bleed. 

Thoughts?

kept.text:
I see a lot of very bullish posts about $META and how this -10% "dip" is an easy buy.

My recommendation: be cautious and really think this one through. We're entering a new paradigm. Many of Meta's customers are small and medium sized businesses that spend a lot of money on advertising to consumers.

AI is eliminating a lot of those businesses and inequality is sadly increasing almost everywhere globally.

This puts META in a very tough where 
a) their customers are under attack
b) their product is getting less valuable

It's not all bad of course. They have great talent. Still print money and have plenty of room to experiment and pivot and also Marc is clearly young and ambitious enough to keep fighting for META until they find their future.

I'm just trying to point out: this is not a "free money" nobrainer dip to buy from my POV.

There are many better risk/reward opportunities in the market. But I get that many people really love this company and stock. The moat their Social Networks have are obviously crazy and they have a lot of incredibly valuable data. The potential of what they could build with that is immense.

profile_bio.description: Founder of Edelbridge Capital. Concentrated public equities fund focused on AI infrastructure. Former pro gamer, YouTuber, and serial entrepreneur.

kept.text:
There are too many people (esp big account) saying $meta earnings is amazing, no red flags, yet price went down. This seems to imply it's a good buy.  I interpret as they dont know what's going on and you should buy.

Seriously you can't base your investment on just numbers itself. Big part of is to anticipate how business evolve.  Simply not trying to understand why is what get you into trouble. There is also a fallacy of safety in numbers (in other words more people buy so it must be safe). Sometimes the biggest is not the safest.

tweet.text:
I have made $META 40% of my portfolio.

Many of you think I'm crazy for this.

But it wasn't so crazy when I had the same approach with $HOOD at $20, was it?

So here's where my conviction comes from.

The AI revolution is quite literally only beginning.

You think you're smarter than a billionaire CEO who has taken $META from $38 to over $600 in 14 years?

A 16x return.

Hate to break it to you, but no, you're not smarter than him.

Zuck is thinking 5 to 10 years ahead. 

Most of you, including Wall St. are thinking about next quarter.

Whoever owns the underlying infrastructure of the most powerful technology ever created by mankind will rule the world.

$META owns the technology. The infrastructure. The distribution.

They have BILLIONS of customers. With a B.

Now here are some data points from yesterdays earnings report:

1. Ad pricing inflected - price per ad rose +12% YoY alongside +19% impression growth, a demand-driven combination per Meta's press release.

2. GEM/Lattice ad stack delivered measurable lifts - CFO Susan Li disclosed +6% conversion rate on landing-page-view ads from Lattice/GEM upgrades, and +1.6% conversion lift across FB/IG from the Adaptive Ranking Model rollout to offsite conversions.

3. GenAI creative tool adoption is now mainstream - more than 8 million advertisers use at least one of Meta's GenAI ad creative tools, with particularly strong SMB uptake.

4. Engagement reaccelerated - Instagram Reels time spent rose +10%, Facebook global video watch time grew +8% (its largest QoQ gain in four years), and FB US/Canada video time +9% in Q1.

5. Business AI surged 10x YTD - weekly conversations on WhatsApp/Messenger jumped from ~1M at start of 2026 to over 10M, per both Zuckerberg and Li.

6. Two big commerce ad products at scale - Partnership Ads hit a $10B run-rate (more than doubled YoY) and the Value Optimization Suite hit a $20B+ run-rate (also more than doubled YoY).

7. AI glasses became a real category - daily users of Meta's AI glasses tripled YoY, Ray-Ban Meta Optics launched this quarter for all-day wear, and Zuckerberg called it "one of the fastest-growing categories of consumer electronics ever".

8. Reality Labs loss narrowed YoY - operating loss improved to -$4.03B vs. -$4.21B in Q1 2025.

9. Capex hike is supply-locking - Li attributed most of the $10B FY guide raise to higher component pricing (especially memory), with $107B in new contractual commitments locking in cloud and infrastructure capacity for 2026–2027.

10. Operating leverage held - operating margin steady at 41% despite 35% expense growth, with headcount at 77,986 (+1% YoY, -1% QoQ) and Li announcing a further employee base reduction in May toward a "leaner operating model"

Hyperscaller şirketlerin bilançolarından anlıyoruz ki Bellek şirketleri hepsinin belini bükmüş :)

2026 için $MSFT - $GOOGL - $META ve $AMZN toplamda 725 milyar $ lık CapEx harcaması açıkladı.

$MSFT - Capex i 190 milyar $ çıkardılar.,  Beklenti 154 milyar $ dı, 35 milyar $ fazla geldi. Bu artışın 25 milyar $ ı sadece donanım zammından, özellikle bellekten geliyor. Komponent zammı diye yeni bir kalem eklemişler bilançolarına :) 
Bu kadar harcıyoruz ama  2026 boyunca kapasitemiz yine yetmeyecek dediler,
Talep devasaymış. ( bunun hakkında yazmıştım daha önce) 

$META - 2026 Capex i 115-135 milyar $ dan 125-145 milyar $ a çıkardı. 
Tüm yönetim bellek fiyatları diyip durdu.
Yönetim… Ne kadar harcarsak, plan yaparsak yapalım çip ihtiyacımız daha fazla oluyor yetmiyor. 

$GOOGL - Yine 190 milyar $ harcayacaklar. Google Cloud %63 büyümüş kâr marjı 2 katına çıkmış. Pichai diyor ki yeterince donanımımız olsaydı çok daha fazla büyürdük. 
Yine Talep devasa ama yeterince çipimiz yok diyip durdular. 

$AMZN - 200 milyar $ harcayacaklar. AWS %28 büyümüş, rekor. 
 Kendi çipini üretiyorlar, Trainium adında 20 milyar $’lık iş yapmışlar. Toplam sipariş 225 milyar $. Trainium2 ve Trainium3 satılmış, Trainium4 bile rezerve edilmeye başlanmış. 
Yönetim diyor ki kendimize ayırdığımız çipleri dışarı satsak 50 milyar $ lık iş olurdu ama içeride kullanıyoruz.

Piyasadaki korkuyu ciddiye almaya gerek yok.

AI infrastructure will grow exponentially for the next 7–8 years.

The message is clear:

AI demand > supply
And it’s not even close.

cloud $AMZN $GOOGL $MSFT
neo-cloud $NBIS $CRWV $IREN $CIFR

is a losing trade.
